rulings.Case Title: GREAT AMERICAN INSURANCE COMPANY OF NEW YORK, A et al VS. U.S. FIRE INSURANCE COMPANY, A DELAWARE et al
rulings.Rulings: Set for hearing on Monday, March 22, 2010, line 9, DEFENDANT U.S. FIRE INSURANCE COMPANY DEMURRER TO FIRST AMENDED COMPLAINT. Demurrer overruled in part, and sustained in part. Demurrer overruled as to the 1st through 4th causes of action. The existence of an actual controversy is properly pled by plaintiffs. A resolution over policy terms is crucial to both parties as it directly implicates when exhaustion will occur and when U.S. Fire will be, if at all, obligated to assume indemnity/defense of team. Hearing required as to remaining causes of action. Court inclined to sustain demurrer without leave to amend as to 5th through 10th causes of action that appear premature since National Surety has not exhausted its policies, and defendant's obligations are not yet triggered. =(302/CWW)
